question: Design a `StringWrapper` class that manages a C-style string (`char*`). Implement a deep copy constructor for `StringWrapper` to ensure proper memory management when objects are copied.

test_cases.input: No direct input, program execution flow.
test_cases.expected:
--- Test Case 1: Basic construction and copy ---
Constructor(const char*) called for: Hello, World!
Copy constructor called for: Hello, World!
s1: Hello, World!
s2: Hello, World!

--- Test Case 2: Empty string and nullptr handling ---
Constructor(const char*) called for: 
Constructor(const char*) called for: nullptr
Copy constructor called for: 
Copy constructor called for: nullptr
s_empty: 
s_null: 
s_copy_empty: 
s_copy_null: 

--- Test Case 3: Objects going out of scope ---
Constructor(const char*) called for: Temporary String
Copy constructor called for: Temporary String
Inside scope - temp_str: Temporary String
Inside scope - temp_copy: Temporary String
Destructor called for: Temporary String
Destructor called for: Temporary String

Program finished. All memory should be properly managed.
Destructor called for: nullptr
Destructor called for: 
Destructor called for: nullptr
Destructor called for: 
Destructor called for: Hello, World!
Destructor called for: Hello, World!
